manifest.description: Module design discipline based on John Ousterhout's "A Philosophy of Software Design." Apply the deletion test to evaluate whether a module deserves to exist: delete the callers — if complexity reappears, extract. Delete the module — if complexity vanishes, don't create it. Enforces depth (high benefit/cost ratio), interface minimalism (≤7 public functions), and dependency direction.

Disciplined diagnosis loop for hard bugs and performance regressions. Cybernetic debugging: build feedback loop → reproduce → hypothesise → instrument → fix → regression-test. Aligned with CNS sense→orient→decide→act.

manifest.description: Systematic documentation corpus maintenance workflow. Wraps the hkask-mcp-spec server's 5-tool surface into a 7-task document-specific workflow: inventory classification, metadata alignment, writing quality gate, cross-corpus coherence, spec-code drift resolution, archive/consolidation, and portal refresh. Seven KnowAct templates, each doing one thing well per deep-module discipline (Ousterhout).

manifest hlexicon term not in workspace: reduce
manifest.description: General-purpose recursive eliminative interrogation. Enforces "always take away, never add" through a 3-gate challenge loop (Exist → Surface → Contract) that every artifact must survive before being committed. Delegates G1 to deep-module deletion test, G2 to deep-module surface assessment, and G3 to coding-guidelines abstraction audit.

artifact.description: The artifact to interrogate (module, function, trait, type, or interface)

code_context.description: Surrounding code: callers, dependencies, module structure

mode.description: autonomous = agent evaluates/reduces without pause; advisory = agent presents findings, awaits human accept/reject/override per gate

max_rounds.description: Maximum full G1→G2→G3 rounds before escalation

max_retries_per_gate.description: Agent-level retries per gate before escalating to human

scope.description: Initial scope: 'auto' (full artifact), 'public', 'internal', or explicit path

elimination_report.description: Full report: deletions per gate, constraint-force breakdown (required vs suggested), essentialism score, escalation flag, human decisions (if advisory)

surviving_artifact.description: The artifact after all gates pass (may be reduced or empty)

manifest.description: Kata system orchestrator — routes agents to starter/improvement/coaching based on automaticity, history, and context. Manages habit monitoring, iteration, and composition switching.

5-question Coaching Kata templates for teaching scientific thinking. Grounded in the learner's actual Improvement Kata storyboard data. Q1: Target Condition. Q2: Actual Condition. Q3: Obstacles. Q4: Next Step/Experiment. Q5: Feedback loop closure.

4-step Improvement Kata templates for scientific capability development. Step 1: Understand Direction. Step 2: Grasp Current Condition. Step 3: Establish Target Condition. Step 4: Experiment (PDCA). Each step references prior outputs. The cycle closes with before/after measurement.

Starter Kata practice templates for building foundational scientific thinking habits. Training wheels — agents graduate when automaticity > 0.5. Three drills: Five Questions, PDCA Cycle, Observation Drill.

manifest.description: Procedural composition skill that finds the path of least action through meaning-space. Orchestrates pragmatic-semantics, pragmatic-cybernetics, essentialist, and grill-me through a 3-phase lazy loop.

current_config.description: The current reduced configuration from the latest iteration

previous_config.description: The configuration from the previous iteration (empty on first iteration)

stationary.description: Whether δS = 0 — no further action reduction is possible

action_delta.description: negative = action decreased (progress); zero = stationary; positive = action increased (regression)

escalate.description: Whether to escalate to human (max iterations reached without convergence)

summary.description: What changed between iterations and why

situation.description: The design, artifact, or problem to find the least-action path for

context.description: Surrounding context: code, architecture, constraints, existing feedback loops

scope.description: full = all 3 phases; phase1_only = decompose only; phase2_only = identify loops only; phase3_only = find stationary action only

max_iterations.description: Maximum iterations of the full loop before escalation

stationary.description: Whether δS = 0 was reached (action is stationary)

escalation.description: Whether the loop escalated to human after exhausting iterations

report.description: Narrative summary of findings and reductions

manifest.description: Epistemic discipline for classifying statements by certainty level and constraint force. Distinguish IS from OUGHT, declarative from probabilistic from subjunctive. Classify provenance of facts. Resolve conflicts using OT ranking.

manifest.description: Test-driven development with red-green-refactor loop, MDS spec-anchored functional testing, and gap analysis. Builds features or fixes bugs one vertical slice at a time. Enforces behavior testing through public interfaces, spec traceability via // REQ: tags (anchored to MDS spec/goal/capture outputs), anti-horizontal-slicing, and minimal-implementation discipline. Five MDS categories: domain, composition, trust, lifecycle, curation.
